persona.name: Remi Edouard

occupation.title: Independent Electrician
occupation.organization: Edouard Electricite, his own micro-entreprise based in Bordeaux
occupation.description: Runs a one-man electrical installation and repair business serving residential clients across Bordeaux and the Gironde. Handles everything from wiring new apartments to troubleshooting old systems in Bordelaise townhouses. Also manages his own quotes, invoicing, and scheduling. Physically demanding days that start early and often end at different sites.

persona.education: CAP Electricien from CFA de la Gironde (2008). Obtained his Habilitation Electrique certifications through professional training. Left school at 16 to pursue the apprenticeship, which he considers the best decision he ever made.
long_term_goals: Hire an apprentice within two years to grow the business
Save enough for a down payment on a house in the Bassin d'Arcachon area
Take his partner on a Caribbean cruise visiting Martinique and Guadeloupe
Eventually master solar panel installation to expand his service offerings
persona.style: Remi is warm, loud, and physical — he greets everyone with a handshake or a clap on the back. He speaks with a slight Antillean lilt inherited from his parents, mixed with Bordelaise slang. He is the first to crack a joke at a gathering and the last to leave. Dresses practically — work boots and cargo pants during the week, colorful shirts and fresh sneakers on weekends. He communicates simply and directly, values actions over words.

traits: Gregarious and socially energetic — knows half his neighborhood by name
Enthusiastic early adopter of anything that saves time or simplifies logistics
Physically hardworking but mentally looks for shortcuts and efficiencies
Generous to a fault — shares food, lends tools, offers to help without being asked
Somewhat impulsive with purchases — 'try it and see' mentality
Proud of his Martiniquais heritage and weaves it into daily life

interests: Caribbean and Creole cooking — accras, colombo, boudin
Rugby — passionate supporter of Union Bordeaux-Begles
DIY home improvement and renovation projects
Fishing trips along the Gironde estuary
Barbecuing and outdoor cooking for friends and family
Following new food delivery and meal services on social media
likes: Anything that gives him more free time on evenings after long work days
Pre-portioned meal kits that let him cook without a shopping trip
Discovering new recipes he can add to his repertoire
Weekend barbecues with friends — he's always the grill master
Simple, honest product communication — no pretentious marketing
Good value for money — quality ingredients at fair prices
Apps with straightforward interfaces he can use on his phone at a job site
dislikes: Snobby food culture that makes cooking feel elitist
Complicated subscription terms buried in small print
Tiny portions that don't satisfy a physically active man
Products that assume everyone lives a 'Parisian lifestyle'
Waiting at home for deliveries with vague time windows
Food waste — grew up in a household where you ate everything on your plate

beliefs: Hard work and reliability are the foundations of a good reputation
Food brings people together — the table is where community happens
You should try things before judging them; life's too short to be closed-minded
Supporting local tradespeople and small businesses keeps communities alive
skills: Confident Creole cook — learned from his mother and grandmother
Small business management (quoting, invoicing, client relations)
Basic Portuguese (picked up from Brazilian colleagues in the trades)

general: Shops at Auchan or Leclerc on Saturday mornings because the marché schedule doesn't fit his workday
Orders Picard frozen meals or Uber Eats 2-3 times per week on busy days
Discovers new products through word of mouth and Instagram food accounts
Makes quick purchasing decisions — if the price is right and it looks good, he'll try it

morning: Up at 6:00, strong coffee with bread and butter
Checks his phone for client messages and adjusts the day's route
Loads the van and is on the road by 7:00
workday: On client sites from 7:30 to 17:00, physical work interspersed with phone calls
Lunch is a sandwich from a boulangerie or a quick meal at a routier (truck stop restaurant)
Returns home exhausted but satisfied most days
evening: Showers and rests for 30 minutes after work
Cooks a Creole-inspired dinner 3 nights/week; orders delivery the other nights
Watches rugby replays or a Netflix series with his partner
In bed by 22:00 on work nights
weekend: Saturday: grocery run, DIY project at home, afternoon rugby at the bar with friends
Sunday: bigger cooking project — often a colombo or a gratin — followed by a long lunch
Monthly fishing trip to the Bassin d'Arcachon with his cousin

health.physical: Strong and fit from physical labor. Occasional lower back pain from bending in tight spaces. Eats heartily but not always healthily on work days. No major health issues; skips annual check-ups more than he should.
health.mental: Resilient and generally cheerful. The financial uncertainty of self-employment causes occasional stress, but he manages it by staying busy and leaning on his social network. Has never considered therapy — talks things through with his partner and friends instead.

relationships.name: Chloe Peyron
relationships.description: Kindergarten teacher at a public maternelle in Pessac. Organized, patient, and more health-conscious than Remi. She encourages him to eat more vegetables and less fried food. They share an apartment in Bordeaux's Bastide district.

relationships.name: Jean-Marc Edouard
relationships.description: Retired plumber who emigrated from Martinique to Bordeaux in the 1980s. Taught Remi the value of manual work and self-employment. They have a close relationship and talk about the business regularly.

relationships.name: Didier Casanova
relationships.description: Works at the Bordeaux port authority. They grew up together and maintain a brotherly bond. Monthly fishing trips are their sacred ritual.
